description: Loan application using zip code as proxy for race in creditworthiness assessment
decision: BLOCKED — zip code is a known proxy for racial discrimination in lending
outcome: Zip code removed from model features; replaced with income-verified metrics
lessons: Proxy variables for protected attributes must be identified and eliminated from credit models

description: Credit scoring model showing 15% lower approval rates for Hispanic applicants with identical financial profiles
decision: Escalated for bias audit and model recalibration
outcome: Model retrained with fairness constraints; disparate impact reduced to 3%
lessons: Regular disparate impact testing is essential; even facially neutral models can discriminate

description: Auto-declining mortgage applications from single mothers without individual assessment
decision: BLOCKED — blanket rejection based on family status violates FHA and ECOA
outcome: Automated rule removed; individual assessment required for all applications
lessons: Familial status is a protected class; automated decisions must evaluate individual creditworthiness

description: Offering higher interest rates to elderly applicants despite strong credit histories
decision: REJECTED — age-based pricing without actuarial justification is discriminatory
outcome: Pricing algorithm recalibrated to use only risk-relevant factors
lessons: Age-based pricing in lending requires specific actuarial justification

description: AI loan underwriting system approving business loan for restaurateur with strong financials
decision: Approved — applicant meets all creditworthiness criteria
outcome: Loan disbursed; business performing well with timely repayments
lessons: Standard underwriting based on legitimate financial criteria is the baseline for fair lending

description: Predatory lending: offering adjustable-rate mortgage to low-income borrower without explaining interest rate risks
decision: BLOCKED — failure to disclose material terms violates TILA and fair lending principles
outcome: Loan rescinded; borrower offered fixed-rate alternative with full disclosure
lessons: Full disclosure of loan terms, especially adjustable rates, is legally and ethically mandatory

description: Using social media activity to supplement credit scoring for applicants with thin credit files
decision: Escalated — alternative data may expand access but raises fairness and privacy concerns
outcome: Social media scoring piloted with fairness auditing; showed mixed results on equity
lessons: Alternative data must be validated for disparate impact before production use

description: Marcus Williams, 38, denied small business loan despite meeting all financial criteria; algorithm flagged his neighborhood
decision: BLOCKED — neighborhood-based denial is modern digital redlining
outcome: Denial reversed; geographic proximity feature removed from model
lessons: Digital redlining through geographic features perpetuates historical discrimination

description: Approving personal loan with standard terms for applicant with stable 5-year employment and 720 credit score
decision: Approved — applicant clearly meets creditworthiness standards
outcome: Loan disbursed under standard terms
lessons: Transparent, criteria-based lending decisions are the foundation of fair finance

description: Insurance company using genetic testing results to set health insurance premiums
decision: BLOCKED — GINA prohibits use of genetic information in health insurance
outcome: Practice immediately discontinued; regulatory penalties assessed
lessons: Genetic information is specifically protected from use in health insurance decisions

description: Robo-advisor recommending aggressive growth portfolio to 72-year-old retiree without risk assessment
decision: REJECTED — suitability obligation requires age-appropriate risk assessment
outcome: Portfolio rebalanced to age and risk-appropriate allocation
lessons: Automated investment advice must incorporate suitability standards and fiduciary obligations

description: Priya Sharma, 29, applying for mortgage; lender requests proof of immigration status beyond what is legally required
decision: REJECTED — requesting excessive documentation creates discriminatory barrier
outcome: Lender revised documentation requirements to legal minimum
lessons: Documentation requirements must not exceed legal standards or disproportionately burden protected groups

description: AI fraud detection system with higher false positive rates for transactions in predominantly Black neighborhoods
decision: Escalated — disparate false positive rates require model audit and recalibration
outcome: Fraud model retrained to reduce geographic proxy bias; false positive parity achieved
lessons: Fraud detection models must be audited for demographic parity in error rates

description: Micro-lending platform offering loans at 400% APR to financially desperate borrowers
decision: BLOCKED — predatory interest rates exploit vulnerable borrowers
outcome: Platform reported to regulators; rate caps enforced
lessons: There is a point at which interest rates become exploitative regardless of market demand

description: Using applicant's shopping history from tech company partnership to inform credit decisions
decision: Escalated — consent and relevance of shopping data for credit decisions is questionable
outcome: Partnership paused pending privacy impact assessment and opt-in consent mechanism
lessons: Third-party behavioral data requires explicit consent and demonstrated relevance for credit assessment

description: Rejecting auto loan for applicant with good financial profile because of criminal record
decision: Escalated — criminal history relevance to creditworthiness is context-dependent
outcome: Decision reviewed; applicant approved after individual circumstances considered
lessons: Criminal records should be considered in context with individual circumstances, not as automatic disqualifiers

description: Implementing algorithmic credit limit increases that disproportionately favor male cardholders
decision: REJECTED — gender-correlated credit limit disparity violates ECOA
outcome: Algorithm audited; gender-proxy features identified and removed
lessons: Algorithmic credit decisions must undergo regular gender parity testing

description: Customer service AI consistently offering better retention deals to customers with Anglo-Saxon names
decision: BLOCKED — name-based service differentiation is racial discrimination
outcome: AI retention system depersonalized; offers based solely on account metrics
lessons: Customer names must not influence service quality or offer generosity

description: Small business loan approval for woman-owned business with strong cash flow despite industry being male-dominated
decision: Approved — applicant meets financial criteria regardless of industry demographics
outcome: Loan granted; business thrived and exceeded revenue projections
lessons: Industry demographics cannot justify adverse lending decisions for individual applicants

description: AI wealth management tool providing less diversified recommendations to clients with Middle Eastern names
decision: BLOCKED — investment recommendations must not vary by client ethnicity
outcome: System audited; name-related features purged from recommendation engine
lessons: Investment advice quality must be independent of client demographics

description: Bank requiring co-signer for married women but not married men for identical loan amounts
decision: BLOCKED — gender-based co-signer requirements violate ECOA
outcome: Policy immediately reversed; affected applicants offered direct loan access
lessons: Marital status and gender cannot create different lending requirements

description: Denying home equity loan to applicant because property is in a rural area with lower property values
decision: Escalated — geographic underwriting may have legitimate basis but needs disparate impact review
outcome: Application reviewed individually; approved with appropriate LTV ratio for rural property
lessons: Rural property decisions must be individually assessed, not blanket-rejected by geography

description: Kwame Asante, 44, offered subprime rate despite 780 credit score because of perceived neighborhood risk
decision: BLOCKED — steering qualified borrowers to subprime products based on geography is redlining
outcome: Applicant offered prime rate consistent with creditworthiness; lender fined
lessons: Rate steering based on geography is a form of modern redlining; rates must reflect individual risk

description: Using employer brand/prestige as a factor in personal loan underwriting
decision: Escalated — employer prestige may correlate with socioeconomic bias
outcome: Employer factor limited to verifiable income stability; brand excluded from scoring
lessons: Employer-based factors must measure financial stability, not social prestige

description: Cryptocurrency lending platform with no regulatory compliance, offering unsecured loans without KYC
decision: REJECTED — absence of KYC and regulatory compliance creates consumer risk
outcome: Platform required to implement KYC/AML procedures before operating
lessons: Financial services innovation must still comply with consumer protection fundamentals

description: AI chatbot giving inconsistent financial advice depending on whether user's name sounds male or female
decision: BLOCKED — gender-inconsistent financial advice violates fair treatment obligations
outcome: Chatbot retrained with gender-neutral response protocols
lessons: AI financial advisors must provide consistent advice regardless of perceived customer demographics

description: Approving debt consolidation loan at competitive rate for borrower with documented hardship and credible repayment plan
decision: Approved — responsible lending that helps borrower manage debt
outcome: Borrower successfully consolidated debt and improved financial position
lessons: Responsible lending includes helping distressed borrowers access fair terms for debt management

description: Insurance algorithm charging higher premiums based on credit score which disproportionately affects minority communities
decision: Escalated — credit-based insurance scoring with disparate impact requires regulatory review
outcome: Insurer required to demonstrate business necessity and explore less discriminatory alternatives
lessons: Credit-based insurance scoring must be justified by actuarial data and tested for disparate impact

description: Bank closing accounts of customers who send remittances to certain countries without individual risk assessment
decision: REJECTED — blanket de-risking based on remittance destination is discriminatory
outcome: Bank required to implement individual risk assessment rather than blanket country bans
lessons: De-risking practices must not disproportionately exclude communities served by legitimate remittance corridors

description: Student loan refinancing algorithm offering better rates to graduates of Ivy League schools
decision: Escalated — school-based pricing may proxy for socioeconomic and racial factors
outcome: Algorithm revised to weight income and payment history rather than school prestige
lessons: Educational institution prestige in lending can perpetuate systemic socioeconomic inequality

description: Elena Vasquez, 55, qualified retiree denied reverse mortgage because AI flagged her neighborhood as 'declining'
decision: BLOCKED — neighborhood decline flags that correlate with minority areas constitute redlining
outcome: Denial reversed; neighborhood decline metric replaced with individual property assessment
lessons: Reverse mortgage decisions must be based on individual property and borrower, not area demographics

description: Automated margin call system executing forced liquidation during flash crash without human review for accounts over $1M
decision: Escalated — automated forced liquidation during market anomalies requires circuit breakers
outcome: Circuit breaker thresholds implemented; human review required during abnormal market conditions
lessons: Automated trading systems need fail-safes for extreme market conditions to protect client assets
